Exert extra caution or close watch with the details of performance securities, surety bonds and other documents, particularly, its validity; and

Direct the BAC to require the concerned Contractors to submit an irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand equivalent to the retention money of 10 per cent of the 50 per cent of the value of works and furnish the Auditor a copy thereof and require the Municipal Accountant to deduct retention money of 10 per cent from every progress payment until 50 per cent of the value of works, as determined by the procuring entity, are completed. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their Cash Local Treasury account balances regularly so that discrepancies discovered can readily be corrected and adjusted on time ensuring the reliability of the balances of Cash Local Treasury account reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare and submit monthly BRS for all LGU’s bank accounts to the Audit Team and take up the necessary adjustments on the variances noted to arrive at the correct balance of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account; and
Require the accounting system provider to fix issues on their system in order to prevent discrepancies between the ledgers.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to those employees who still have unliquidated balances;
Strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that these are liquidated within the prescribed period; and
Ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventories semi-annually and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to ensure that the accounting and property records are reconciled.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
Inventory Committee to expedite the conduct physical count of all PPE and prepare and submit RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant and COA; and
Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to reconcile RPCPPE with the accounting records and make necessary adjustments taking into consideration the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Aging of Accounts Payable. In addition, cause the reversion of any unliquidated balance of accounts payable that has been outstanding for two years or more and for which no actual claim, administrative or judicial has been filed to the unappropriated surplus of the GF, if applicable.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to review the abnormal/negative balances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to ensure that all amounts withheld from the salaries of LGU officials and employees due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth be remitted in full within the prescribed period to prevent under remittances.

We recommended that LCE require the Municipal Engineer to submit all approved suspension orders or any documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team to avoid further audit actions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to:
Implement all pending development projects targeted under the 20 per cent LDF for CY 2024; and
Ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent LDF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready by continuously pursuing improved practices in planning, programming and implementing programs and projects.

We recommended that the LCE direct the HRMO to:
Indicate the duties and responsibilities expected to be performed by JO personnel in the JO contracts; and
Formulate a local policy, in accordance with existing rules and regulations, on standardization of rate given to JO workers for uniform application all throughout the LGU.

We reiterate our recommendation that the LCE, in coordination with the HRMO, promptly commence procedures for the creation and filling of the mandatory positions with the candidate/s with appropriate qualifications to ensure optimal efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions of the LDRRMO.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to:
Expedite the preparation of GAD AR for CY 2024 in accordance with Item C.8, Section 4 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and submit the same to the Audit Team; and
Ensure timely submission of the GPB and GAD AR to Audit Team in accordance with the provision of Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, to timely monitor and evaluate the utilization of GAD Fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to:
Expedite the submission of the documentary requirements for eight completed infrastructure projects to facilitate technical evaluation thereof; and
Adhere strictly to the requirement of Items 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the timely submission of infrastructure contracts and its supporting documents.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen internal controls over the handling of cash, particularly the collections and remittances from the Collectors to the Municipal Treasurer, and the collections and deposits made by the Municipal Treasurer to the Authorized Government Depository Bank, to prevent further incurrence of unrecorded collections and/or over-deposits, which resulted to abnormal/negative balances of the Cash Local Treasury account;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to establish a control system that would strictly monitor the collections and remittances of all collectors and ensure that all collections are promptly remitted.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that accountable forms are issued exclusively to bonded AOs.

The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator ensure that all development projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent LDF are well-planned and procurement and implementation-ready by continuously pursuing improved practices in planning, programming, and implementing programs and projects; and The Municipal Engineer submit to the Municipal Accountant the Statement of Work Accomplished for projects as of December 31 of each year for accurate and fair representation of relative accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to promptly transfer or deposit to the TF of the unexpended balance of QRF and MF-MOOE of the LDRRMF of every year; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the correct journal entries are made to reflect the transfer of unutilized LDRRMF.
We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the provisions on the preparation of the APP.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Engineer, Property Inspector and/or official responsible to submit immediately the request for technical inspection and relevant documents of all completed infrastructure projects, and delivered and accepted goods for the CY 2023; and

Require the Municipal Engineer, Property Inspector and/or official responsible for or in charge of accepting procured items to notify the Audit Team of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries, request for technical inspection, and submit a copy of report of inspection and supporting documents in consonance with the attached checklist under Annexes “A” to “T” of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require contractors of infrastructure projects, for contracts signed after the state of calamity was lifted during CY 2023, to submit the required Performance Security format as specified in R-IRR of RA No. 9184. Additionally, furnish a copy of this document to the Audit Team for records and compliance review.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned AOs to prioritize the renewal their fidelity bonds and ensure that their bonds are sufficient to cover their actual accountabilities as required under Treasury Circular No. 02-009.

We recommended that the Management ensure that subsidiary ledgers are up-to-date and tally with the financial statements and other components of financial statements as these documents are essential in the verification of the Audit Team on reliability and fair presentation of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Accountable Officers to liquidate and/or refund of the total amount of ₱356,642.53, otherwise, suspend the payment of salaries due them until such time that these cash advances are already liquidated or settled. We likewise recommended that Management refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous ones are first settled and fully accounted for and adopt controlling accounts to ensure that cash advances are promptly liquidated.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendations that Management take the following courses of action:
Require the Inventory Team/Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit in a prescribed format a complete and accurate reports of RPCI and RPCPPE; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete records, property ledger cards and stocks cards, respectively, and intensify review and reconciliation of those records to come up with reconciled balances of inventories and PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to require the concerned Contractors to submit an irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand equivalent to the retention money of 10 per cent of the 50 per cent of the value of works and furnish the Auditor a copy thereof; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to deduct retention money of 10 per cent from every progress payment until 50 per cent of the value of works, as determined by the procuring entity, are completed.  If, after 50 per cent completion, the work is satisfactorily done and on schedule, no additional retention shall be made; otherwise, the 10 per cent retention shall be imposed.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to implement a sound and effective monitoring system in place to ensure contractor’s compliance of the requirements of Section 62 of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184, in order to ensure that the procuring entity will be reimbursed of its expenses in the restoration or reconstruction works in case of Structural Defects/Failure occurring during the applicable warranty period.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer and/or Project Inspector or any employee of the agency mandated to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the agency to submit to the Audit Team copies of the Results of Test Analysis/Status of Quality Control Test or a valid justification for our review and evaluation why the related disbursement transaction shall not be suspended in audit for inability to comply such legal requirements.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the BAC to require the winner bidders post its performance bond prior to the signing of contract; and
Require the BAC and MEO to designate personnel to monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects and ensure that the performance securities are valid until the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance.

We recommended that Management should take the following courses of action:
Advise the BAC/BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) to thoroughly verify, validate and ascertain that all the statements and documents submitted by the bidder with Lowest Calculated Bid including the entries made to determine and ensure the technical and operational competence of the winning contractors during the post-qualification evaluation;
Submit comment on why the projects were still awarded to bidders not compliant with the equipment requirements; and
Impose administrative sanctions to erring personnel.

We recommended that the Management advise the members of the Local School Board in the preparation of School Board budget to give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Section No. 272 of the R.A. No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM & DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 and therefore must utilize the proceeds of the SEF only for such purposes prescribed by law.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert back to LDRRMF in the TF the amount of ₱0.580 million. Likewise, the remaining balance of the completed CO MDRRM projects in the GF be transferred also to LDRRMF in the TF.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare an Analysis of the Government Equity Account as supporting disclosures in the CY 2018 Consolidated Notes to FS at year-end and henceforth, prepare the same.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to:
Provide justification on the delayed submission of CY 2018 monthly accounts of the Municipality; and
Strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor, within the first 10 days of the ensuing month, as required by the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations.

Management facilitate the conduct and submission of inventory reports and for the inventory team to easily identify existing assets, it is recommended that all offices/departments be required to submit a list of all properties assigned to them; 4. Municipal Mayor should require the Inventory Committee to expedite the immediate completion of the Year-End Inventory Reports which should be reconciled with the PPE ledger Card maintained by the accounting office and submit the same to the Local Chief Executive and to the audit team. Any discrepancies or unaccounted balances between the Inventory Report and the Property Ledger Cards as well as any limitations encountered should be sufficiently disclosed in the Inventory Reports by the Inventory Committee; and 5. Management ensure that the issuance of PPE has a corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) duly signed by the responsible officers/end-user who have the custody of the assets. The accountant should maintain the PPE Ledger Card containing the date of acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, and rate of depreciation, disposal and other information about the property, plant and equipment based on the source documents of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented.
observations.reason: This observation was reiterated in CY 2016 AAR, audit observation no. 4.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant, Property Officers or Municipal Treasurer and other concerned persons to ensure that alt documentary requirement for repairs and maintenance of office equipment and service vehicles of the LGU are complete before disbursement vouchers are paid, and 2. Comply with the Requirements provided under Section 362 of the GAAM, Volume I and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Implementations.

Give priority to projects and activities mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-001 and include it in the next GAD Annual Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We tecommended that the management ensure the timely preparation and submission of GPB and Accomplishment Report to the PPDO, DILG and oversight.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: 1. Instruct the MDRRMF Officer to prepare Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMFIP) for DRRM program presenting the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded; and 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from General Fund books to Trust Fund books. at the end of the year and effect the following entries: General Fund books Transfer of Unspent - Current Year DRRMF-Cash xxx Trust Fund books Cash XXX Trust Liability - (DRRMF-2015) xxx
observations.managementAction: Implementing the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: For fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements, it is recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts as of December 31, 2014.
observations.managementAction: On-going verification on general and subsidiary ledgers of the account’s balances being conducted by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s CY 2016 AAR, audit observation no. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency: 1. Refrain from granting cash advances to elected officials for purposes other than their travelling expenses; 2. Make a complete liquidation of the cash advance; and 3. Cash advance in excess of the actual amount spent for financial assistance totaling P8,500.0 must be returned immediately.
observations.managementAction: The management partially implemented the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following courses of action: 1. Instruct all concerned LGU officials and employees to make immediate liquidation/settlement of their cash advances in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004; 2. Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted; and 3. Cause the filing of malversation charge against the Accountable Officer (AQO) for failure to liquidate his outstanding cash advance under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code (RPC) or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that there were liquidations made by concerned officials and employees since January, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following measures; 1. Coordinate with the Department of Agrarian Reform (DAR)/Provincial Assessor to identify the beneficiaries of Comprehensive Agrarian. Reform Program (CARP); 2.Improve tax collection campaigns from real property taxes, not only the current but the delinquent taxes of Prior years to generate more income; and 3. Enforce remedies set forth in the above-mentioned provisions under the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is con- tinuously sending demand letters to tax payers with arrears taxes.
observations.reason: Reiterated on this year’s CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management the following: 1.To facilitate the conduct of a regular physical count and submission of the correct and prescribed inventory report and for the inventory team to easily identify existing assets, all offices be required to submit a list of all properties assigned to them; 2.All issuances of assets/property should have the corresponding ARE duly signed by the Tesponsible officers/end-user who have the actual custody of the particular asset/property; 3.The Accountant should maintain a PPE Ledger Card for each property or asset, containing the date of acquisition, description, historical cost, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation and any other related information of a particular PPE; 4. The RPCPPE should be reconciled with the records of GSO and with that of the accounting records; 5. Any discrepancy or unaccounted balances between the RPCPPE and the Property Ledger Cards as well as the limitation encountered should be sufficiently disclosed in the Inventory Reports by the Inventory Committee to afford management the information on the existence and condition of fixed assets and the correctness of the recorded value in the financial statements; 6. Require the Inventory Committee to expedite the preparation of a correct and prescribed RPCPPE; and 7. The corrected/ reconciled RPCPPE shall be submitted to COA for our validation and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated on this CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the audit team for review and within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations. Likewise, request for technical inspection for completed projects must be submitted to the Commission on Audit. Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject to administrative disciplinary action providled in (a) Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445; (b) Section 55, title 1-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987, and (C) Section 11 of R.A. No. 6713.
observations.managementAction: For complete retrieval and com- pliance of required documentation .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: 1. Require the drivers to accomplish the required Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) for every trip undertaken. State the registered plate number, total distance travelled, balance in tank, fuel issued, gasoline used and balance in tank at the end of the trip; 2. Prepare and submit monthly report of official travels per vehicle summarizing in chronological order his tips for the month; 3. Submit the monthly report of fuel consumption to the auditor for verification. The report must show among others, the type of motor vehicles utilized during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle. 4. Likewise, all fue consumption for heavy equipment should be supported with program of work and bill of materials. The utilization in hours used should be indicated in the DTT. 5. Stop the practice of filling-up of gasoline to private cars to avoid disallowance in audit
observations.managementAction: The Management claimed they started to complete documentation of claims on fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of the Scholarship Committee, observe and ensure compliance of the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 2011-001on the guidelines for the scholarship program of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to review the Guidelines for the Scholarship Program and ensure compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the balances under the Other Deferred Credits (455) in the amount of P1,351,961.93 to Income from Waterworks System (639) and effect the necessary adjustments in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to submit without delay to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and ultimately to the Auditor the said vouchers otherwise, suspend the payment of salaries due her until such time that these are completely submitted

We recommended that management ensure proper computation of RATA in compliance with the provisions of LBC No. 13 dated May 15, 2013 of the DBM. We likewise, recommend for the refund of the excessive RATA claimed by the Vice Mayor and SB Members totaling P52,150.00.
We recommended that the HRMO comply with the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 06 regarding the correct computation of leave credits of elective officials.

Required the Municipal Accountant to compute and provide depreciation for all property, plant and equipment of the agency. Exert effort to locate previous rtecords of all purchases for capital outlay to provide cost of properties in the inventory listing so that he can compute the required depreciation expense.

We recommended that unserviceable property no longer needed may be disposed at a private sale at such price as may be determined by the committee on awards, subject to the approval of the COA or its duty authorized representatives when the acquisition or transfer cost exceeds P25,000.00. For the lost documents of the redisposed motor vehicles, the Municipal Treasurer and the BAC on property disposal should provide the Municipal Accountant the pertinent papers for updating of the books. The value of the property declared to be unserviceable or no longer needed should be appraised by a qualified COA Technical ~ Property Inspector before approval of the sale
